sourceName: Sultan Mehmood, Andreas Goessmann and Elliott Ash
title: A nationwide trial finds training determines whether JudgeGPT helps
friendlyTitle: AI helped judges more when they also received training
summary: A randomized field study involving 1,559 judges across Pakistan tested JudgeGPT, a system for searching, citing and summarising precedents and case documents. The researchers report a 6.3% increase in case resolution overall, with gains concentrated among judges who also received hands-on training rather than tool access alone.
friendlySummary: A large study in Pakistan found that JudgeGPT helped courts complete more cases, mainly when judges learned how to use it properly.
whyItMatters: The result suggests that deployment support can matter as much as access to a model in high-stakes professional work. Courts and other public services should budget for training, quality measurement and appeal safeguards instead of treating an AI subscription as a complete reform.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Giving people an AI tool is not enough. Training and careful checks are important, especially for decisions that affect lives.
